D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

The 25 MacAdam Ellipses

slide the magnification and watch 25 just-noticeable-difference ellipses swell across the chromaticity chart

Around any colour there is a region the eye cannot tell apart from it. Plotted on the CIE diagram these regions are ellipses, and their size and tilt vary dramatically with position.

The real ellipses are far too small to see, so slide the magnification to inflate them. Notice the tiny tight ellipses in the blue corner and the sprawling ones in green — colour space is not uniform.
